questions.question: How many total orders are in the database?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS total_orders
FROM order_details;
questions.error:
Query error: syntax error at or near "LIMIT"

LINE 3: LIMIT 1000
        ^

questions.question: How many unique users have placed orders?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users
FROM order_details;
questions.error:
Query error: syntax error at or near "LIMIT"

LINE 3: LIMIT 1000
        ^

questions.question: What is the average number of days between orders for repeat customers?
questions.sql:
SELECT AVG(days_since_prior_order) AS avg_days_between_orders
FROM order_details
WHERE days_since_prior_order IS NOT NULL

questions.question: What is the most common hour of day for placing orders?
questions.sql:
SELECT order_hour_of_day, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM order_details
GROUP BY order_hour_of_day
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1

questions.question: How many orders were placed on Saturdays?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS saturday_orders
FROM order_details
WHERE order_dow = 0

questions.question: What is the maximum number of items ever added to a single order?
questions.sql:
SELECT MAX(items_in_basket) AS max_items_in_order
FROM (
    SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS items_in_basket
    FROM order_details
    GROUP BY order_id
)

questions.question: What are the top 10 most frequently purchased products by name?
questions.sql:
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(*) AS appearances
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY appearances DESC
LIMIT 10;

questions.question: How many distinct products appear in the order_details table?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT product_id) AS distinct_products
FROM order_details

questions.question: What are the top 5 most reordered products?
questions.sql:
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(*) AS total_orders, SUM(reordered) AS total_reorders, ROUND(AVG(reordered), 4) AS reorder_rate
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 100
ORDER BY reorder_rate DESC
LIMIT 5;

questions.question: Which products are most commonly added first to the cart?
questions.sql:
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(*) AS times_added_first
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
WHERE od.add_to_cart_order = 1
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY times_added_first DESC
LIMIT 1000;

questions.question: How many orders contain at least one reordered item?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS orders_with_reordered_items
FROM order_details
WHERE reordered = 1

questions.question: What is the average basket size (items per order)?
questions.sql:
SELECT AVG(items_in_basket) AS avg_basket_size
FROM (
    SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS items_in_basket
    FROM order_details
    GROUP BY order_id
)

questions.question: Which department has the highest reorder rate?
questions.sql:
SELECT d.department, AVG(od.reordered) AS reorder_rate
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
GROUP BY d.department
ORDER BY reorder_rate DESC
LIMIT 1

questions.question: What are the top 5 aisles by total number of purchases?
questions.sql:
SELECT a.aisle, COUNT(*) AS total_purchases
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
GROUP BY a.aisle
ORDER BY total_purchases DESC
LIMIT 5;

questions.question: Which department has the largest average basket size?
questions.sql:
SELECT d.department, AVG(basket.items) AS avg_basket_size
FROM (
    SELECT od.order_id, od.product_id, p.department_id
    FROM order_details od
    JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
) sub
JOIN departments d ON sub.department_id = d.department_id
JOIN (
    SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS items
    FROM order_details
    GROUP BY order_id
) basket ON sub.order_id = basket.order_id
GROUP BY d.department
ORDER BY avg_basket_size DESC
LIMIT 1

questions.question: What percentage of purchases in the produce department are reorders?
questions.sql:
SELECT
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(od.reordered) / COUNT(*), 2) AS reorder_percentage
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE d.department = 'produce'

questions.question: Which aisle has the highest average cart position?
questions.sql:
SELECT a.aisle, AVG(od.add_to_cart_order) AS avg_cart_position
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
GROUP BY a.aisle
ORDER BY avg_cart_position DESC
LIMIT 1

questions.question: Show the top 10 products by purchase count in the beverages department.
questions.sql:
SELECT p.product_name, COUNT(*) AS purchase_count
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE d.department = 'beverages'
GROUP BY p.product_name
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

questions.question: Which day of the week has the most orders? Return the day name.
questions.sql:
SELECT
    CASE order_dow
        WHEN 0 THEN 'Saturday'
        WHEN 1 THEN 'Sunday'
        WHEN 2 THEN 'Monday'
        WHEN 3 THEN 'Tuesday'
        WHEN 4 THEN 'Wednesday'
        WHEN 5 THEN 'Thursday'
        WHEN 6 THEN 'Friday'
    END AS day_name,
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders
FROM order_details
GROUP BY order_dow
ORDER BY total_orders DESC
LIMIT 1

questions.question: How many orders are placed between 9am and 12pm?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS orders_between_9am_and_12pm
FROM order_details
WHERE order_hour_of_day BETWEEN 9 AND 12

questions.question: What is the average days between orders for users who have placed more than 5 orders?
questions.sql:
SELECT AVG(days_since_prior_order) AS avg_days_between_orders
FROM order_details
WHERE days_since_prior_order IS NOT NULL
  AND user_id IN (
    SELECT user_id
    FROM order_details
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) > 5
  )

questions.question: How many users place orders every week (days_since_prior_order <= 7)?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users_ordering_weekly
FROM order_details
WHERE days_since_prior_order IS NOT NULL
  AND days_since_prior_order <= 7
GROUP BY user_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) = SUM(CASE WHEN days_since_prior_order <= 7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)

questions.question: What is the overall reorder rate across all purchases?
questions.sql:
SELECT AVG(reordered) AS reorder_rate
FROM order_details

questions.question: What proportion of first-time product purchases (reordered=0) are in the produce department?
questions.sql:
SELECT
    ROUND(
        SUM(CASE WHEN d.department = 'produce' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 / COUNT(*),
        4
    ) AS produce_proportion
FROM order_details od
JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE od.reordered = 0

questions.question: What is the average number of items in a basket for orders placed on Sundays?
questions.sql:
SELECT AVG(items_in_basket) AS avg_basket_size_sunday
FROM (
    SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS items_in_basket
    FROM order_details
    WHERE order_dow = 1
    GROUP BY order_id
) basket_sizes

questions.question: Which products have the highest reorder rate among users who order weekly?
questions.sql:
WITH weekly_users AS (
    SELECT user_id
    FROM order_details
    WHERE days_since_prior_order IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY user_id
    HAVING AVG(days_since_prior_order) BETWEEN 6 AND 8
),
weekly_user_orders AS (
    SELECT od.product_id, od.reordered
    FROM order_details od
    INNER JOIN weekly_users wu ON od.user_id = wu.user_id
),
product_reorder_rates AS (
    SELECT
        product_id,
        COUNT(*) AS total_appearances,
        SUM(reordered) AS total_reorders,
        ROUND(AVG(reordered), 4) AS reorder_rate
    FROM weekly_user_orders
    GROUP BY product_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 50
)
SELECT
    p.product_name,
    a.aisle,
    d.department,
    pr.total_appearances,
    pr.total_reorders,
    pr.reorder_rate
FROM product_reorder_rates pr
JOIN products p ON pr.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN aisles a ON p.aisle_id = a.aisle_id
JOIN departments d ON p.department_id = d.department_id
ORDER BY pr.reorder_rate DESC
LIMIT 1000

questions.question: How many unique products appear in the training set?
questions.sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT product_id) AS unique_products
FROM order_details
WHERE eval_set = 'train'

questions.question: What is the reorder rate in the prior set versus the train set?
questions.sql:
SELECT
    eval_set,
    AVG(reordered) AS reorder_rate,
    COUNT(*) AS total_items
FROM order_details
WHERE eval_set IN ('prior', 'train')
GROUP BY eval_set
ORDER BY eval_set
